title: Investigate False Positive Field Deleted Reports in History Tracking
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want field history tracking to accurately report deleted fields, so that I can trust the change log.
description:
Field history tracking was reporting fields as deleted when no fields had actually been removed from Salesforce. The false positives may have been caused by deleting and re-adding an object in DataTools Pro, which would reset the local field list and cause a comparison mismatch on the next refresh.

### Investigation
Waqar investigated and found that the deletion detection compares the current Salesforce field set against the locally stored fields. If fields were removed from the local database (e.g., by deleting the object), the comparison would see the re-fetched fields as "new" but might also flag previously stored fields as "deleted" if timing was off.
acceptance_criteria: Field history does not report false positive deletions
Deleting and re-adding an object does not produce false deletion records
middleware_changes: Investigation into field comparison logic during object refresh to eliminate false positive deletion reports.
implementation_notes: Root cause likely related to object delete/re-add resetting local field state. Waqar requested a meeting to investigate further in the customer's org.
comment_summary: Waqar investigated but couldn't reproduce. He suggested the deletion detection was working as designed — comparing local vs Salesforce fields. Requested a meeting for further investigation.
